records.ety: [AS. crecca; akin to D. kreek, Icel. kriki crack, nook; cf. W. crig crack, crigyll ravine, creek. Cf. Crick, Crook.]

defs: A small inlet or bay, narrower and extending further into the land than a cove; a recess in the shore of the sea, or of a river.
quotes: Each creek and cavern of the dangerous shore.
They discovered a certain creek, with a shore.

defs: A stream of water smaller than a river and larger than a brook.
quotes: Lesser streams and rivulets are denominated creeks.
The passages of alleys, creeks, and narrow lands.
